title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: The over priced liberal arts and private universities are the schools that gave huge scholarships. They ended being the lowest cost. So if you're a top student DO apply to those!

title: Colleges you need to avoid...

author: @malcorub

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: Correction to the private vs public schools point: private colleges often offer generous financial aid that can make it cheaper to attend than a state school. State schools offer offer almost no institutional aid since they don't have the large endowments of private and are already offering such a reduced tuition to begin with. If you are interested in a non profit accredited private college then go ahead and apply and see how much fin aid they offer you and what the conditions to receive it are. Don't write off private colleges as "too expensive" just by looking at the sticker price of cost of attendance because almost nobody besides the super rich actually pay that amount.
author: @first9299

author: @warwick802

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: I'm 26 years old and will be attending a private university in Austin, Texas this fall semester! (I'm really excited after returning back to my home city from new student orientation). I avoided college until mainly because I lacked the structure from not understanding what I'd like to do as a career. Honestly I wasn't aware of the way private universities work (or at least the school where I'm a student at). I just avoided private schools due to the cost. It blows my mind about how opposite my personal case worked out! Summary: 50,000 a year. I received federal grants, 3 school grants, then applied for the 2 small loans my fafsa could offer. My total per semester as a full time on campus student with meal plan is five thousand a semester. That's it. I can more than work with only 10K a year if it means I get to be a part of that campus I've already found my belonging with. My lack of research could have made this happen years earlier ago for me, but once admissions got into contact with me I've gained many big new insights. That is how I found this video because I'm so interesting in learning more about this new concept in my life lol
author: @BrookePainter

Thanks really find your videos helpful and definitely been doing some of the things you talk about

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: The point about finding a college that supports you is spot on.  Not just for finding a career afterward, but guiding you through your academics for the time you're attending.  Ask current students; how is your academic advisor?  Are they proactive in catching problems before they get too bad?  Regular scheduled meetings during the school year?  Is there tutoring and essay writing assistance located in the library?  Some schools just leave you to fend for yourself; they graduate students who swim and discard those who sink.
author: @bridgecross

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body:
One sidebar as someone who has worked in the tech & software industry for over 25 years: one of the biggest shocks to me was discovering how over-rated many students of so called 'Ivy League' or prestigious schools actually are. And I am not saying this as someone who is bitter about not attending such a school. It was never in the cards for me. But I have to admit, that did surprise me. Schools like Stanford, Harvard, Yale, etc all carry tremendous prestige. But honestly, I can't think of a single person I ever worked with who graduated from any of those schools that impressed me in the workplace. Harvard MBAs are in my opinion, the most over-rated. Generally speaking, I think that degree is better suited for people that want to work on Wall Street. But the mindset doesn't mesh well with the corporate sector. Most of those people only focus on the numbers, but don't have the slightest idea how to actually run a company. And their decisions are often predicated on what is best for them, as opposed to what is best for the company.
I will say that there is one prestigious school that has graduates that I have always admired and enjoyed working with; and that would be MIT. I've had several colleagues who graduated from that school and not only were they exceptionally smart, they were also very amicable and humble. I think the big difference is it is a school that really just focuses on merit and seems to have a positive vibe associated with it. That translates into graduates who are personable and are more in it for their love of technology and science.
author: @Diomedes01

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body:
The one about schools with 'bad reputations' is a little bit sketchy. Often schools that aren't well liked only become unpopular through tribalism politics or rumors that grew too large for the truth to be heard. Speaking from personal experience, I ended up going to a school that was thought of as a school for the slower students because many people chastised it for a) offering dumb degrees and b) having a high acceptance rate. When I did some digging I found out that the employment rate for my degree was high, that students went on to do their Masters in good schools and that the actual difficulty of the degree was no different than any other degree in my country. The school did offer a few off shoot wonky degrees, and while the acceptance rate was fairly above average, it wasn't as high as its reputation implied. The drop out rate was considerably high for first year STEM students though.

Reputations are often misleading.
author: @thereccher8746

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: my cousin got into NYU and Brown but it was so expensive. So instead he chose Kings College London, UK which ranked 35th globally and cost 70% less. If anyone is interested in going to a good University i would suggest not just looking in USA but also Europe. The major advantage is that the undergraduate degree is just 3 years and someone can get masters degree as well for far less cost.
author: @greatindiandreams

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body:
Ok this is the way I am doing it
1. I took many AP and Dual credit classes during my High School time
2. I went to my local community college for a Summer and 2 semesters.
3. I transferred to a University less than an hour away from my parents house, so I could stay with them.
I am currently in my penultimate semester, expected to graduate in May of 2024, and so far I have only spent $14,824 in tuition (I just need to pay 4,275 for my last semester and that’s it)
author: @AlexRamirez-nj7bk

title: Discover Rutgers University—New Brunswick
body: To get a better picture of Rutgers, ask any student who goes there or alumni. Ask about the "R U Screw," grading fairness, whether the faculty knows your name, and cleanliness of the campus. All that with some amazing metrics like research done by faculty (not much for undergraduates) and faculty authorship of peer-reviewed journals. And the salary of faculty. It may be a better place to teach than attend as a student.

title: Discover Rutgers University—New Brunswick

I'm 36 years old, and I have a bachelor's degree and a master's degree in literature, both from Italy. Only now do I have the opportunity to move to another country.
I've been told that Rutgers is an excellent university, and I'd like to pursue a bachelor's degree in Italian literature.
But I have many doubts. I don't know whether to pursue this dream, and I don't know what steps I need to take to become a Rutgers student. Can anyone give me some advice? Thanks.

title: why i transferred // Seton Hall to Rutgers
body: I can still remember my first day of class at Rutgers, and getting scared off by all of the people boarding the EE bus at the bus stop by College Hall on Douglass. That plus a bad roommate situation in New Gibbons (I was at Douglass College…that was before the colleges merged) caused me to originally withdraw from the school. After my father forced me to go back and get reinstated, I started fresh the following semester living in Jameson and getting involved with a community service fraternity called Alpha Phi Omega (they’re still active on campus). It gave me a sense of community and made a huge university feel a little smaller. I eventually graduated with a Psychology major and a music minor.
author: @dorothysewing9997

title: why i transferred // Seton Hall to Rutgers
body: Rutgers Outdoors club was also the best decision I made in college. Hopefully we can have some socially distant hikes this semester although it’ll be hard.
author: @videowatcher4852

title: why i transferred // Seton Hall to Rutgers
body: Great video, Emily! I definitely can understand your situation! I too was unhappy and uncomfortable with my initial undergraduate school that I attended. I decided to leave because it was just too small for my liking. It really wasn't for me. I then transferred to Arizona State University and it was the best decision that I ever made. I graduated Summa Cum Laude and as a result, landed a terrific job. Sometimes,  experiencing something that you ultimately find isn't for you, points you to what you really want. There's nothing wrong with this. It's part of life! Glad that you found what is best for you! What's ironic though, is that I'll be attending graduate school at Rutgers this fall! Be happy and I love your videos!!

title: why i transferred // Seton Hall to Rutgers
body: Just the thought of going to college for $40,000+ in tuition per year really freaks me out. Private or not, the amount of money that colleges charge people is exploitive not to mention, it keeps going up! I mean, can you imagine having it in the back of your mind that you have to pay back all of that money now during covid where so many people are not working, can barely put food on the table much less can pay their rent/mortgage??  That said, when I go to college I am going to know EXACTLY what I want even if it means that I end up going a year or two later than everybody else.

title: A Day in the Life of a Cardiac Surgeon
body: These men and women are the true heroes of the world. Their dedication, professionalism and great skills are truly remarkable.
author: @beaverman011

title: A Day in the Life of a Cardiac Surgeon
body:
As an OR nurse, this doctor is pure efficient. You can tell he also treats his coworkers with respect and stays very humble.

This is what a good doctor looks like.
author: @LarsVilksIsAwesome

title: A Day in the Life of a Cardiac Surgeon
body: Dr Lee replaced my aortic valve a year and a half ago. He is a wonderful surgeon, physician, and human being. I owe my life to him and the entire team at RWJ Barnabas Medical Center.
author: @dankaiser5282

title: A Day in the Life of a Cardiac Surgeon
body: Hi Dr Lee!  You did a double bypass on me on 12/11/2020 other then a little rib cage pain I feel great!  I can walk over 2 miles without feeling wiped out.  I can't wait to start riding my Bike!  Have a Great Day and People don't be afraid, I am 53 and feeling great again!
author: @derykmacleod4979

title: A Day in the Life of a Cardiac Surgeon
body: Only a few cardiac surgeons in the world have the same quality and speed of work as Dr Lee! He is as pleasant to work with on and off camera!!
author: @pantin_enrique

title: A Day in the Life of a Cardiac Surgeon
body: I am a minimally invasive robotic surgeon in his early 40’s. When I was in medical school at Cornell, some 15 years ago, I had the pleasure to watch Dr Lee perform a CABG. He is an absolute beast. A surgical GOD, if there ever were one. He’s precise and extremely efficient. To this day, I have never witnessed anyone operate like him. He is masterful. Like poetry in motion.
author: @jasserthiara5789

title: A Day in the Life of a Cardiac Surgeon
body:
Lowly pre-med here currently trying to get in med school. I must say, Dr. Lee looks like the man. Would love to be a cardiac surgeon like him one day. Thank you all so much for the video!!

EDIT: just got accepted into medical school, will be an MD soon!!
author: @patrickk5030

title: A Day in the Life of a Cardiac Surgeon
body: I am so happy!! I am a medical student and just found out that I was accepted for a placement in cardiac surgery for a uni hospital in Switzerland, literally my dream work place that I was so anxious it wouldn’t work out!! So excited for the future!!!!
author: @christine2866

title: A Day in the Life of a Cardiac Surgeon
body: Having been a little off my game today at my desk job, I cannot even comprehend this level of responsibility every single day.
author: @Rodgort42

title: A Day in the Life of a Cardiac Surgeon
body: Doctor has the 3Cs qualities - Calm, Cool & Collected. He is the unshakeable even when the world around him falling apart
author: @sarala1jan

title: A Day in the Life of a Cardiac Surgeon
body: I love that Dr. Lee explains what he’s doing in such a way that a layman like myself can understand what he’s talking about. He’s clearly a fantastic doctor and more importantly, human being.
author: @SJM6791

title: A Day in the Life of a Cardiac Surgeon
body: It feels great when awesome doctors like him, recognize that’s it’s never only their glory and their work that need to be congratulated. Anesthesiologists doctors and their fellow nurses, Us, the nurses in the General ICU, the heart surgeons. Everyone needs to be congratulated 😊 It’s a team work and that’s why I love it.
author: @UnKnownUser123-i2o

title: A Day in the Life of a Cardiac Surgeon
body: So glad they didn't show any of the surgery, literally would have passed out haha. Bless this man for what he does. Absolute legend
author: @FalconProductions_

title: A Day in the Life of a Cardiac Surgeon
body: 2:00...a standard phone call, one he probably makes a dozen times a week, to let a relative know a "minor" surgery went well...however never underestimate the relief felt by the relative on the phone. You can tell by the way the woman gasps...this guy is one of the many true heroes of this world
author: @cavedweller2000

title: day in a life of an Indian student in USA 🇺🇸

title: day in a life of an Indian student in USA 🇺🇸
body:
You woke up 6:30am and went to bed at 9:00pm 😅
Me: wakes up 8:00am and sleeps at 1:00 am😂😂
author: @praveenpusapati2264

title: day in a life of an Indian student in USA 🇺🇸
body: I’m Indian and it makes me feel disappointed in our Indian people because when they see someone doing better than them in life, they get jealous, envious and negative. They start making fun of that person. Gargipawar, you’re a good person. Don’t take these losers seriously. They are upset that you are in USA and they don’t have the same opportunity as you do. Ignore them! Do well in life regardless of these negative people and their hate and you keep progressing forward in all areas of your life. Make your parents proud. Good luck! ❤
author: @igo7434

title: day in a life of an Indian student in USA 🇺🇸
body: Why are you guys even bothering it's her choice to be in there  maybe she has friends your not some friend to tell which place she has to go and maybe it that bad or good

body: I am an Indian student as well. You don't need to wake up at 6:30 unless you take forever to get ready.

title: day in a life of an Indian student in USA 🇺🇸

title: 🩵🏫My First Day at University | Maryam Masud Studying Computer Science Honors| Rutgers Uni New Jersey
body: I don't know why this video made me so emotional. I've watched Mariam grow and recite the Quran beautifully for years. May Allah SWT continue to guide and protect you from the evil of dunya and akhira. May Allah SWT continue to bless your entire family and reward your parents with the best of rewards for raising you on the straight path. We love you Mariam and you make me cry. From a Muslim sister from West Africa.

title: 🩵🏫My First Day at University | Maryam Masud Studying Computer Science Honors| Rutgers Uni New Jersey
body: আমার বাংলাদেশর অহংকার, দোয়া রইলো অবিরাম অন্তহীন, পৃথিবীর বর্তমান প্রেক্ষাপটবিবেচনায়  তোমার জন্য খুব টেনশন হয়, আল্লাহ তোমাকে নেক হায়াত দান করুক, নিজের প্রতি খুব খেয়াল রাখিও মা আমার, পরিশেষে দোয়া রইলো  আল্লাহ যেন সকল বিপদ থেকে তোমাকে রক্ষা করে, আমিন।

title: A realistic day in the life of a nursing student
body: If our instructors knew we didn’t get at least 7-8 hours of sleep. They send us home. Some girls can’t talk about their schedules around them, they’ve gotten in trouble 😢
author: @studyosa

title: A realistic day in the life of a nursing student
body: oh my gosh i start clinical next year and im so excited but im also very scared cuz i know imma have a terrible sleep pattern😭😂
author: @Fluffly_Panda

title: A realistic day in the life of a nursing student
body: Could you make a video of how you got into nursing because I want to go into nursing just want to know a little bit more ❤❤ love you videos
author: @myaderbidge4989

title: A realistic day in the life of a nursing student
body: I can say as a nurse who graduated in 2018, I still do the same thing and feel a lil anxious before day shifts. Regularly get 4.5hrs of sleep and then bam off for a busy, rewarding, always interesting 12hr day 🤗😋💗 I know a few other nurses who are like this too, others do get into a routine of going to bed early, so hopefully maybe one day lol ❤😋🤗 I sleep in before my night shifts, but can’t nap through the day, helps me to sleep at least 4hrs after my night though so that is helpful for recovery 🤗💗
author: @haileytasker8899

title: A realistic day in the life of a nursing student
body: For the people saying "How is it realistic, she sleeps with the lights on? Bud, she turns on the lighs in the beginning of the video🤦‍♀️

body: Shortest Clinical day I ever had was 8 hours but that was bc the mental hospital was an hours drive away and they didn’t want us to have to deal with traffic 😅
